queries: tag triggered github actions release workflow
CI workflow self-hosted gauntlet runner golangci-lint

build_from.reason: The only existing tag-triggered workflow in the repo. It is explicitly `based_on: .github/workflows/ci.yml` and already solves the exact problem the new release workflow needs solved: scoping a tag-push trigger so it does not collide with a different tag namespace, reusing the [self-hosted, gauntlet] runner, Go 1.25 via actions/setup-go, and the pinned repo-root .golangci.yml.
build_from.suggested_approach: Create a new `.github/workflows/release.yml` on: push: tags: 'v*' (bare vX.Y.Z, deliberately disjoint from reconcile-module.yml's 'reconcile/v*' filter so the two workflows never both fire on the same tag). Reuse the checkout/setup-go steps verbatim, then add a goreleaser-action step gated on the tag push.

existing_patterns.name: Tag-namespaced release-gate workflow
existing_patterns.description: .github/workflows/reconcile-module.yml triggers only on `reconcile/v*` tags specifically so it never fires on an ATCR app tag (`v1.2.3`). This is a deliberate, tested design from Epic 8.0, not incidental.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new release workflow's tag filter must use the disjoint 'v*' namespace and should call out in a comment (mirroring reconcile-module.yml's own header comment) why the two filters never collide. The tested precedent is recorded in .planning/sprints/completed/8.0_reconciler_library/plan/acceptance-criteria/06-01-tag-push-release-gate-workflow.md (Edge Case 1: 'Tag filter scopes to module releases, not ATCR app tags').

existing_patterns.name: based_on CI reuse convention
existing_patterns.description: New tag-triggered workflows in this repo declare `based_on: .github/workflows/ci.yml` in a header comment and copy the runner label, checkout, and Go setup steps verbatim rather than inventing new tooling.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new release.yml should follow the same reuse pattern: [self-hosted, gauntlet] runner, actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-go@v5 with go-version '1.25', cache: false.

existing_patterns.name: ldflags version stamping (already scaffolded, currently split across two variables)
existing_patterns.description: Two separate, currently unconnected version variables exist: (1) internal/version.Version (var Version = "0.0.0", internal/version/version.go:16) used only in the leaderboard submission envelope per Epic 10.0's clarification decision, stamped via -X github.com/samestrin/atcr/internal/version.Version=..., and (2) cmd/atcr's own package-local `var version = ""` (cmd/atcr/version.go:14) which is what atcrVersion()/root.Version actually use for `atcr version` / `atcr --version` output, stamped via -X main.version=..., falling back to debug.ReadBuildInfo()'s module version, then VCS revision, then "dev".
existing_patterns.follow_for: AC2 needs the goreleaser ldflags config to stamp BOTH `-X main.version=` (drives `atcr version`/`--version`) and `-X github.com/samestrin/atcr/internal/version.Version=` (drives the leaderboard submission envelope) from the same tag, or the two will silently diverge (e.g. a `go install` build reporting a stale/derived version in the CLI while the leaderboard envelope reports something else). This corrects the epic's own Refinements advisory #2, which claimed both already read a single internal/version.Version — cmd/atcr/version.go does not read that package at all.

related_files.purpose: Defines Version var (default "0.0.0") used by the leaderboard submission envelope (Epic 10.0). Comment already documents the -ldflags stamping convention.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only (no code change needed — AC2 is a build-time wiring gap, not a code gap) but MUST be included in the goreleaser ldflags list

related_files.purpose: Defines the package-main-local `version` var and atcrVersion() resolution logic actually used by `atcr version` / `atcr --version`.
related_files.likely_modification: reference-only — MUST also be included in the goreleaser ldflags list (-X main.version=...) alongside internal/version.Version

related_files.purpose: newRootCmd() sets `Version: atcrVersion()` (line 136) so cobra auto-registers --version; the atcr version subcommand mirrors the same string.
The only existing tag-triggered GitHub Actions workflow in the repo; establishes the tag-namespace-scoping and CI-reuse conventions the new release workflow must follow.

The base CI workflow (`based_on` target) — runner label, Go setup, golangci-lint version pin (v2.12.2 as of reconcile-module.yml; ci.yml action pin drifted to v8 with an inline version arg) all originate here.

NOT tag-triggered, so it cannot collide with release.yml's bare 'v*' tag filter — listed here for inventory completeness so a reviewer sees the full .github/workflows/ set when checking for tag-trigger collisions.

Pinned to mutable major-version action tags (actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-go@v5) — same pinning style the new release workflow will inherit via the based_on ci.yml convention. Listed for workflow-inventory completeness.

This single file is AC1's sole evidence for the convention; there is no separate archive file (docs/CHANGELOG_archive.md is deprecated — never tracked, ghost index hit).

The full epic-number-as-semver history — [1.0.0] (CHANGELOG.md:2082) through [20.1.0] (CHANGELOG.md:1) — lives in the single CHANGELOG.md (2095 lines); no separate archive file exists. Use CHANGELOG.md as AC1's sole evidence.

related_files.purpose: The tested acceptance-criteria precedent for a tag-scoped release-gate workflow. Its 'Edge Case 1: Tag filter scopes to module releases, not ATCR app tags' is the design decision the new release.yml's disjoint bare 'v*' filter must honor; also defines the permissions: contents: read posture the module gate uses.

The original decision record creating internal/version.Version specifically so a release build could stamp it via -ldflags (-X github.com/samestrin/atcr/internal/version.Version=...), and the rationale for the neutral "0.0.0" default. Source of truth for WHY integration_points[0] is an ldflags target.
Module path github.com/samestrin/atcr, go 1.25.0 — confirms the Go toolchain version the release workflow and goreleaser config must target.

integration_points.description: Release build must set -X github.com/samestrin/atcr/internal/version.Version=<tag-without-v-prefix-or-with, TBD by AC1 decision>
Release build must ALSO set -X main.version=<same value> so `atcr version`/`--version` and the leaderboard envelope never diverge
New tag-push trigger scoped to bare 'v*', running goreleaser to build cross-platform binaries and publish a GitHub Release

reusable_components.name: [self-hosted, gauntlet] CI runner + Go 1.25 setup + golangci-lint pin
reusable_components.usage_example: Copy the checkout/setup-go steps verbatim into the new release workflow before invoking goreleaser, per the based_on convention already used by reconcile-module.yml

architecture_notes: The tag-namespace decision is already effectively made and battle-tested: bare vX.Y.Z for ATCR app releases, reconcile/vX.Y.Z reserved and already live for the nested ./reconcile module. The two workflows' tag filters must remain disjoint.

Version stamping is a two-target problem, not a one-target problem: internal/version.Version (leaderboard envelope) and cmd/atcr's local version var (CLI output) are currently independent variables with no shared source of truth. A release build's ldflags must set both from the same tag value or the CLI and the envelope can silently disagree.

CHANGELOG.md already carries an unbroken epic-number-as-semver convention — 20+ entries from [1.0.0] (line 2082) through [20.1.0] (line 1), 2095 lines total, with zero git tags cut against any of them — this epic's versioning decision is about formalizing an existing, already-numbered history, not inventing a fresh counter. (No separate archive file exists; docs/CHANGELOG_archive.md is a deprecated ghost reference.)

No goreleaser config, no release-focused doc, and no tag-triggered ATCR-app workflow exist anywhere in the repo today. .github/workflows/ holds four workflows — ci.yml (push/PR), reconcile-module.yml (push, tag-scoped to reconcile/v*), hermes-auto-merge.yml (pull_request), refresh-synthetic-manifest.yml (schedule + workflow_dispatch) — none of them an ATCR-app release, and only reconcile-module.yml is tag-triggered at all, so bare 'v*' is free. This is greenfield packaging infrastructure layered onto an established CI-reuse convention.
